blurb:
This is hands down one of the best dinner spots in the area, and I'm not exaggerating. It's in the Aria complex and has this warm, upscale vibe that's perfect for girls' night out, date night, or celebrating something special. The service is incredible and they make you feel like you're the only table that matters.

Start with the hummus and definitely try the chicken shish kabobs with that rice that has lentils and raisins. The wings are surprisingly amazing too. The cocktails are creative and strong, and save room for the tiramisu because it's worth the splurge.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner service, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But when you want a meal that feels like an event, this is where you go. My friends and I are already planning our next visit.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is your chance without driving into the city. They bring different grilled meats right to your table on skewers, and you control the pace with a little green and red card. It's fun for the family and actually a pretty good deal if you're really hungry.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip. They make it fresh and bring it out warm throughout your meal. The feijoada stew is authentic and hearty, perfect for cooler weather. Don't skip the salad bar either because they have some Brazilian specialties you won't find elsewhere.

Come hungry because you're paying for the full experience whether you eat a little or a lot. They stay open later on weekends if you want to make it a Saturday night thing.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is the real deal French bakery that makes everything from scratch daily. The owners are actually French and you can tell the difference in their pastries and bread. It's become my Saturday morning ritual to grab coffee and a croissant here, especially when I want to feel a little fancy without the fancy prices.

The quiche changes daily and it's always perfect for lunch. Their baguettes are what you'd expect in Paris, and the petits fours are almost too pretty to eat. If you're doing brunch, the French toast made with their brioche is incredible.

They close at 6pm most days and 3:30pm on Sundays, so don't plan on late afternoon visits. But for morning pastries or weekend brunch, this place beats anything you'll find at chain bakeries.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
My kids discovered this place and now we're regulars. It's real Italian thin-crust pizza, not the thick American stuff, and the quality is seriously impressive. The crust is perfectly crispy and they use ingredients that actually taste like something. Good for family dinner or date night since it's not too loud or chaotic.

The traditional Italian pizzas are where they shine, and even my picky eaters find something they like. The salads are also really good if you want something lighter to share. It's become our Friday night go-to when we don't feel like cooking.

It's in that Blue Stone shopping area, so parking is easy and you can grab groceries after dinner if you need to. The 4.9 rating isn't a fluke, this place really delivers.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is the place that saves you when nothing else is open. It's been a Marietta institution forever, with that classic neon sign and 24-hour service that actually means something when you need late-night food or really early breakfast. The Greek family that runs it knows how to do both diner classics and their own specialties.

The breakfast menu is huge and they don't mess around with portion sizes. Get the Greek omelet if you want something different, or stick with their pancakes if you're feeding kids. The gyro platter is surprisingly good for a diner, and honestly better than some Greek places I've tried.

Just know it gets busy on weekend mornings, so either go really early or be prepared to wait. But hey, they're open 24 hours, so you've got options.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I'm craving proper Indian food but don't want to deal with the crowds at some of the bigger places. It's in that shopping center near Perimeter Mall, and the inside has this colorful, family-friendly vibe that makes it perfect for introducing kids to Indian food or just a casual dinner with friends.

The biryani is obviously what they're known for, and it's legit. The portions are huge and meant for sharing, so come hungry or plan on leftovers. My wife always orders the traditional curries, and they do a good job with spice levels if you're not used to the heat.

They're open later on weekends which is clutch if you want Indian food after 9pm, something that's harder to find around here.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This place is legit and the hype is real. It's in that Sandy Springs Place development, and the vibe inside is energetic but not crazy loud. Perfect for group dinners, post-game meals with the kids, or just when you want really good Mexican food that isn't the usual chain stuff.

Start with the Esquite because it's addictive, and definitely try one of their pitcher cocktails if you're with a group. The Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones are both solid and perfect for sharing. The tacos are obviously the main event and they don't mess around with quality.

They're open until midnight most nights which is huge when you want good food late. The service is fast and friendly, so it works whether you're doing a quick family dinner or hanging out with friends for a couple hours.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is where we go for anniversaries, special occasions, or when we want to impress visiting family. The location right on the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially if you can get a table by the windows. It's definitely a splurge, but the kind of place that makes the evening feel special.

The seafood is what they do best, and everything feels elevated without being too fancy to enjoy. The atmosphere strikes that perfect balance between upscale and comfortable. Great for date nights or celebrating something important.

Just know it's pricey and you'll want reservations, especially on weekends. But when you want that special dinner out without driving all the way into Buckhead, this is the spot.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This little spot in a Franklin Road strip mall serves some of the best Middle Eastern food around, and the prices won't hurt your wallet. It's nothing fancy inside, but the family running it clearly knows what they're doing. Perfect for when you want something different but don't want to drive all the way into Atlanta.

The shawarma is what most people come for, and they pile it high with fresh vegetables and sauce. The falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside, and they make their own pita bread. If you're vegetarian, this place has more options than most restaurants around here.

They close at 9pm every day, so don't plan on late dinner here. But for lunch or early dinner, it's become our go-to when we want something healthy and flavorful.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is genius for when your group can't agree on what to eat. It's a food hall with twenty different vendors all under one roof, so everyone can get what they want and still sit together. Way better than the mall food court and right on the Square so you can walk around after.

There's everything from tacos to Asian food to pizza, plus some local vendors selling things like honey and coffee beans. The kids love being able to pick from different places, and we adults appreciate having craft beer available. Good for family dinners when everyone wants something different.

Parking can be tricky on weekend evenings when the Square gets busy, but it's worth circling the block once or twice. They stay open until 10pm on weekends, so you don't have to rush through dinner.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
